Patch Set 1:

I don't think this is entirely true. nrs_delay is used to simulate network issues so its not a server only thing. The rest of the NRS policies are server specific. Chris Horn wrote this so he can say for sure.

Patch Set 1:

Trevis Autotest2 started enforced review-ldiskfs-dne session for build 78518.

Patch Set 1:

> I don't think this is entirely true. nrs_delay is used to simulate
 > network issues so its not a server only thing. The rest of the NRS
 > policies are server specific. Chris Horn wrote this so he can say
 > for sure.

Right, the intent is to perturb timing of request handling on servers as well as clients (like for lock callback requests).

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Passed enforced test review-ldiskfs-dne on CentOS 7.8/x86_64 uploaded by Trevis Autotest2 from trevis-211vm1: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/de8c900d-4cbe-45ca-b4c9-f4287a03971c ran 5 tests.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Need to make NRS delay available for the client as well.

Patch Set 1:

> Right, the intent is to perturb timing of request handling on servers as well as clients (like for lock callback requests).

That might be the intent, but it is not what the code says.
If you build without HAVE_SERVER_SUPPORT, then call 
  ptlrpc_nrs_policy_register(&nrs_conf_delay);
is not made, so nrs_delay code is not used at all.

Are you saying that your patch  https://review.whamcloud.com/14701 was wrong?  Should that policy_register() call be outside the #ifdef?
Uploaded patch set 2.

Looks good to me.

parents.subject: LU-14285 utils: Add error message when osd_init fails

commit.subject: LU-14291 ptlrpc: support nrs_delay for client-only builds
commit.message:
LU-14291 ptlrpc: support nrs_delay for client-only builds

nrs_delay is intended for use on the client, but is currently disabled
for client-only builds.  So move the ptlrpc_nrs_policy_register() call
outside of the #ifdef.

Also change the nrs modules that *are* server-only to not have #ifdef
in the C file, but that rather to be identified in the Makefile as
only being built when server code is wanted.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Mr NeilBrown <neilb@suse.de>
Change-Id: Ie25ed5ac837ff5f891824fad31ab258923840ec0
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/41127
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
Reviewed-by: Chris Horn <chris.horn@hpe.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
